senses.definition: a false or spurious sense of parallelism in rhetoric or reasoning
examples: The speaker relied on pseudoallelism to create a sense of balance without real logical parallelism.
Scholars dismissed the passage as pseudoallelism due to its shaky structural symmetry.

senses.definition: a term used to describe a non-genuine or imitation alum, often in chemical or material contexts
examples: The lab labeled the compound as pseudoalum to indicate its nonstandard composition.
Researchers questioned whether the pseudoalum crystals possessed the same properties as true alum.

senses.definition: not true or accurate to the alveolar region or anatomy; appearing to relate to the alveolar area but fictitious or distorted
examples: The diagram provides pseudoalveolar measurements that do not match actual anatomy.
His description offered pseudoalveolar features rather than real structural details.

senses.definition: characteristic of someone who pretends to be an amateur but shows unskilled or awkward behavior
examples: The painting looked pseudoamateurish, with rough strokes and uncertain technique.
Her pseudoamateurish attempts at cooking fooled no one familiar with kitchen basics.

senses.definition: in a way that imitates amateurish behavior or skill in a deceptive or exaggerated manner
examples: He spoke pseudoamateurishly, overemphasizing misunderstandings to seem humble.
The review praised the work for not dressing up its flaws pseudoamateurishly.

senses.definition: the condition or quality of appearing amateurish without genuine competence
examples: The film suffered from pseudoamateurism, with obvious budget constraints and clumsy execution.
Critics noted the project’s pseudoamateurism as a deliberate stylistic choice.

senses.definition: of or relating to a false or deceptive form of amateur practice or behavior
examples: The exhibit featured pseudoamatorial elements that mimicked craftsmanship without real skill.
Herpseudoamatorial tendencies were evident in the overconfident yet flawed performance.

senses.definition: describing text or speech that pretends to be sentimental or amorous but is not genuine
examples: The letter carried a pseudoamatory tone, masking a more practical intention.
Readers sensed a pseudoamatory flourish that felt manufactured.

senses.definition: pretending to be ambidextrous or capable with both hands, but not truly skilled with both
examples: The pseudoambidextrous painter favored one hand, despite the outward claim of dual dexterity.
His pseudoambidextrous claims faded when he attempted to cut with the other hand.

senses.definition: in a way that pretends to be ambidextrous but is not genuinely skilled with both hands
examples: He waved pseudoambidextrously, though his left hand was clearly less capable.
The coach watched pseudoambidextrously, noting habits that favored his dominant hand.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of a pseudoameba (an organism resembling an amoeba) in appearance or behavior, but not truly amoeboid
examples: The sample showed pseudoameboid movement under the microscope, unlike true amoebae.
Pseudoameboid shapes were observed in the gel but did not indicate living cells.

senses.definition: appearing to belong to a different historical period than the present; not truly in the correct era
examples: The film used pseudoanachronistic dialogue that felt out of place for the setting.
The design included pseudoanachronistic elements that confused viewers about the time period.

senses.definition: in a manner that imitates pseudoanachronism, i.e., falsely implying an older or different era
examples: The costume was worn in a pseudoanachronistical way, hinting at a different century than the story's setting.

senses.definition: resembling or resembling an anaphylactic reaction but not caused by a true allergen exposure
examples: The patient experienced pseudoanaphylactic symptoms without a genuine allergic trigger.
Clinicians noted pseudoanaphylactic responses to the test substance.

senses.definition: a condition resembling anaphylaxis but not caused by a true allergic reaction
examples: She suffered a bout of pseudoanaphylaxis after exposure to the scent, though she had no allergy history.
Differentiating pseudoanaphylaxis from real anaphylaxis is critical in emergency care.

senses.definition: resembling anarchistic ideas or movements but not truly anarchistic in intent or scope
examples: The manifesto contained pseudoanarchistic rhetoric that aimed to provoke rather than organize.
Critics described the group as pseudoanarchistic, lacking genuine structure or goals.

senses.definition: not truly anatomical; resembling anatomy but incorrect or misleading
examples: The model showed pseudoanatomic features that did not match real human anatomy.
Pseudoanatomic diagrams can lead to misunderstandings in education.

senses.definition: of or relating to a false or misleading form of anatomy
examples: The chart presented pseudoanatomical data that did not correspond to real tissue structures.
Researchers warned against pseudoanatomical interpretations of the imaging results.

senses.definition: in a way that imitates or misrepresents anatomical structure
examples: The illustration was pseudoanatomically labeled, causing confusion among students.
The figure was pseudoanatomically accurate at a glance but incorrect upon closer inspection.

senses.definition: pretending to be ancestral or relating to ancient lineage, but not truly
examples: The artifact was described as pseudoancestral, lacking authentic provenance.
Researchers questioned the pseudoancestral claims in the exhibit.

senses.definition: in a way that imitates ancestral characteristics or lineage but is not genuine
examples: The narration presented pseudoancestrally, layering myth over documented history.

senses.definition: a condition that resembles anemia in appearance or symptoms but is not due to true anemia
examples: The patient was diagnosed with pseudoanemia after tests ruled out actual iron deficiency.
Pseudoanemia can be caused by dehydration or other non-hematologic factors.

senses.definition: resembling anemia but not actually anemic
examples: The blood work showed pseudoanemic readings that improved with hydration.
She felt weak, but the doctor noted pseudoanemic markers rather than true anemia.

senses.definition: appearing angelic or virtuous but not truly so; superficially good
examples: The leader's pseudoangelic facade hid ruthless decisions.
Her pseudoangelic charm masked a calculating mind.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling pseudoangelical qualities; not genuinely angelic
examples: The speech carried a pseudoangelical tone that aimed to comfort yet manipulated.
